title: Verify T-Echo L76K PPS connectivity
description: Determine from authoritative schematic, PCB, module, test-point, or direct manufacturer evidence whether the current LilyGO T-Echo routes the L76K 1PPS output to the nRF52840, exposes it on a test pad/header, or leaves it unconnected. Repository DTS evidence currently shows only UART1 TX/RX and is insufficient.
acceptance_criteria: Record exact T-Echo hardware revision and L76K variant; cite manufacturer-grade schematic/PCB/pin evidence; identify PPS pin, net, MCU GPIO or test point, voltage domain, and any buffer/series components; identify EXTINT, GNSS power-enable, and reset/wake routing while inspecting the same evidence; clearly conclude connected, accessible-only, unconnected, or unknown; update the capability assumption without claiming UART/NMEA as PPS.
notes: Conclusion: CONNECTED in the documented classic T-Echo U1.0 design and T-Echo Plus pin-compatible design. Official LilyGO T-Echo commit 9de74b0dd86bb2793b5dfd26c35341f8ffaaa0b1 README lists GPS L76K PPS=P1.04 and says T-Echo/Plus share pinout. Official examples/GPS/utilities.h defines Gps_pps_Pin=(1,4); examples/Factory/gps.cpp configures it as input; Factory.ino polls it in the PPS LED path. Published Altium schematic blob 131f79277affa35e4efc5308e26865f105109b1d (397184 bytes; creation metadata 2021-06-23) shows L76K pin 4 1PPS through 1 kOhm series resistor onto PPS net terminating at nRF52840 P1.04. Official pin-map image for PCB marking U1.0 also labels PPS P1.04. Meshtastic firmware commit 0199a1f3ffea1d82ff23c5931df74b459a17a5b8 independently defines PIN_GPS_PPS=(1,4). Wake is P1.02, reset P1.05, peripheral rail enable P0.12, V_BCKP is board backup power. No dedicated PPS connector/test-point is documented for classic/Plus; an accessible unlabeled via is unknown because Gerbers are unpublished. Separate family variants: T-Echo-Lite V1.0 routes PPS to P1.15 and external header pad 2; T-Echo-Card V1.0 routes PPS to P0.23. Production caveat: schematic contains alternate Air530/L76K populations and public evidence cannot prove every batch or a specific physical unit; inspect module marking/PCB revision and perform continuity plus dual-channel PPS/P1.04 scope capture. Precision suitability remains unproven until fixed offset/jitter and hardware timer capture are measured. Discovered separate likely LICHEN UART direction bug project-LICHEN-pg54.
close_reason: Confirmed from pinned official LilyGO schematic, pin map, README, GPS/factory firmware, and independent Meshtastic pin definitions that documented classic T-Echo U1.0 and T-Echo Plus route GNSS 1PPS through 1 kOhm to nRF52840 P1.04. No dedicated classic/Plus PPS test point is documented; production-batch and precision behavior remain for manufacturer confirmation and physical validation in 2nnd.5.5. Updated capability matrix and filed pg54 for the separately discovered reversed GNSS UART mapping.
